title: centaurea langei subsp. exilis
intro: Centaurea langei subsp. exilis, commonly known as Lange's knapweed, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. It is a flowering plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating gastrointestinal issues, due to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
